req.title: Open savings account (boundary variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-027 mandates a savings account creation endpoint with a $100 minimum balance and automatic tier assignment. Test design covers: (1) EP positive — a mid-range valid deposit to confirm happy-path account creation and tier assignment; (2) NEG negative — a deposit below $100 to confirm 422 rejection; (3) BVA boundary-min — exact $100 deposit to confirm acceptance at the lower threshold; (4) BVA boundary-below-min — $99.99 to confirm rejection one cent below threshold; (5) DT multi-condition — a high-balance deposit to confirm a higher interest tier is automatically applied; (6) NEG precondition negative — an unverified customer is rejected, heuristic corrected from STC to NEG because it exercises an invalid precondition partition rather than a state-transition sequence. Together these cases give full coverage of the equivalence partitions, boundary pairs, decision rules, and error pathways described in the requirement.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_mid_range_deposit
preconditions: customer has a valid auth token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an interest_tier field (non-null)

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_deposit_returns_422
preconditions: customer has a valid auth token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error detail referencing minimum balance

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_exact_minimum_deposit_accepted
preconditions: customer has a valid auth token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an interest_tier field (non-null)
trace: R-FIN-027: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_one_cent_below_minimum_returns_422
preconditions: customer has a valid auth token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body references minimum balance constraint
trace: R-FIN-027: boundary behaviour — deposit just below min threshold

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_high_balance_receives_premium_tier
preconditions: customer has a valid auth token
initial_deposit = 50000.00 (qualifies for highest tier per rate schedule)
API rate schedule defines a premium tier threshold (e.g. >= 10000)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: interest_tier value corresponds to the highest defined tier (e.g. 'premium' or tier_3)
trace: R-FIN-027: tiered interest rates applied automatically
DT: condition combination — verified customer AND high deposit => premium tier

tests.name: test_unverified_customer_cannot_open_savings_account
preconditions: customer has an auth token but is NOT verified
actions: POST /accounts/savings with body {"initial_deposit": 500.00} using unverified customer token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error indicating verification is required
trace: R-FIN-027: precondition — customer.verified = true is required
NEG: invalid precondition partition — unverified customer is rejected before business logic executes
R-FIN-027: error pathway — unverified actor must not open account
